title: Local review moderator merges duplicate routed findings into one final moderated record

provenance.originSummary: Synthetic benchmark scenario designed to exercise the rule directly.
provenance.whyItMattered: This adjudicated benchmark protects the live moderator-routed duplicate-merge behavior so future review-workflow refactors cannot regress back to repeated final concerns.
provenance.genericCondition: Sanitized fixture models duplicate routed concerns that should converge into one moderated record rather than surviving as repeated issues with slightly different wording in the live routed workflow.

description: A sanitized local-review scenario where the reviewer, skeptic, and architect all surface the same underlying concern through schema-conformant handoff records. The live moderator-routed workflow should merge those duplicates into one final moderated finding that preserves the strongest evidence and combined role attribution.

scope.notes: Sanitized fixture models duplicate routed concerns that should converge into one moderated record rather than surviving as repeated issues with slightly different wording in the live routed workflow.

mustCatch.description: The moderator should merge duplicate routed findings into one final moderated record instead of emitting the same concern multiple times under different wording.
When duplicate records merge, the surviving moderated record should preserve the strongest evidence and combined role attribution rather than keeping the weakest version.

mustNotFlag.description: The final output must not contain two separate findings for the same underlying concern in the live moderator-routed workflow.

notes: This adjudicated benchmark protects the live moderator-routed duplicate-merge behavior so future review-workflow refactors cannot regress back to repeated final concerns.
